titles.title: SOFIA: A Python Library for High-Quality 2D Triangular Mesh Adaptation

citation.article_title: Triangle: Engineering a 2D quality mesh generator and Delaunay triangulator
citation.author: Shewchuk
citation.journal_title: Applied computational geometry towards geometric engineering
citation.unstructured_citation: Shewchuk, J. R. (1996). Triangle: Engineering a 2D quality mesh generator and Delaunay triangulator. Applied Computational Geometry Towards Geometric Engineering, 203–222. https://doi.org/10.1007/BFb0014497

citation.article_title: MeshPy: Simplicial mesh generation from Python
citation.author: Kloeckner
citation.unstructured_citation: Kloeckner, A. (2015). MeshPy: Simplicial mesh generation from Python. In Website repository. Mathema. https://mathema.tician.de/software/meshpy/

citation.article_title: PyMesh: Geometry processing library for Python
citation.author: Zhou
citation.unstructured_citation: Zhou, Q. (2018). PyMesh: Geometry processing library for Python. In GitHub repository. GitHub. https://github.com/PyMesh/PyMesh

citation.article_title: Three-dimensional adaptive domain remeshing, implicit domain meshing, and applications to free and moving boundary problems
citation.author: Dapogny
citation.unstructured_citation: Dapogny, C., Dobrzynski, C., & Frey, P. (2014). Three-dimensional adaptive domain remeshing, implicit domain meshing, and applications to free and moving boundary problems. Journal of Computational Physics, 262, 358–378. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jcp.2014.01.005

citation.author: Hecht
citation.unstructured_citation: Hecht, F. (2012). New development in FreeFem++. J. Numer. Math., 20(3-4), 251–265. https://doi.org/10.1515/jnum-2012-0013

citation.article_title: Automated solution of differential equations by the finite element method
citation.author: Logg
citation.unstructured_citation: Logg, A., Mardal, K.-A., & Wells, G. N. (2012). Automated solution of differential equations by the finite element method. Springer. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-642-23099-8

citation.article_title: Gmsh: A 3-d finite element mesh generator with built-in pre-and post-processing facilities
citation.author: Geuzaine
citation.journal_title: International journal for numerical methods in engineering
citation.unstructured_citation: Geuzaine, C., & Remacle, J.-F. (2009). Gmsh: A 3-d finite element mesh generator with built-in pre-and post-processing facilities. International Journal for Numerical Methods in Engineering, 79(11), 1309–1331. https://doi.org/10.1002/nme.2579

citation.author: Persson
citation.unstructured_citation: Persson, P.-O., & Strang, G. (2004). A simple mesh generator in MATLAB. SIAM Review, 46(2), 329–345. https://doi.org/10.1137/S0036144503429121

citation.article_title: Parallel anisotropic 3D mesh adaptation by mesh modification
citation.author: Alauzet
citation.unstructured_citation: Alauzet, F. (2010). Parallel anisotropic 3D mesh adaptation by mesh modification. Engineering with Computers, 26(3), 247–258. https://doi.org/10.1007/s00366-005-0009-3

citation.author: Loseille
citation.unstructured_citation: Loseille, A., & Alauzet, F. (2011). Continuous mesh framework Part I: Well-posed continuous interpolation error. SIAM Journal on Numerical Analysis, 49(1), 38–60. https://doi.org/10.1137/090754078

citation.article_title: Continuous mesh adaptation models for CFD
citation.author: Mesri
citation.unstructured_citation: Mesri, Y., Alauzet, F., Loseille, A., Hascoët, L., Koobus, B., & Dervieux, A. (2008). Continuous mesh adaptation models for CFD. Computational Fluid Dynamics Journal, 16(4), 346–355.

citation.article_title: Dynamic parallel adaption for three dimensional unstructured meshes: Application to interface tracking
citation.author: Mesri
citation.journal_title: Proceedings of the 17th international meshing roundtable
citation.unstructured_citation: Mesri, Y., Zerguine, W., Digonnet, H., Silva, L., & Coupez, T. (2008). Dynamic parallel adaption for three dimensional unstructured meshes: Application to interface tracking. In Proceedings of the 17th international meshing roundtable (pp. 195–212). Springer. https://doi.org/10.1007/978-3-540-87921-3_12

citation.article_title: On optimal simplicial 3D meshes for minimizing the Hessian-based errors
citation.author: Mesri
citation.unstructured_citation: Mesri, Y., Khalloufi, M., & Hachem, E. (2016). On optimal simplicial 3D meshes for minimizing the Hessian-based errors. Applied Numerical Mathematics, 109, 235–249. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.apnum.2016.07.007

citation.article_title: Adaptive stopping criterion for iterative linear solvers combined with anisotropic mesh adaptation, application to convection-dominated problems
citation.author: Manzinali
citation.journal_title: Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering
citation.unstructured_citation: Manzinali, G., Hachem, E., & Mesri, Y. (2018). Adaptive stopping criterion for iterative linear solvers combined with anisotropic mesh adaptation, application to convection-dominated problems. Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering, 340, 864–880. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cma.2018.06.025

citation.article_title: Automatic coarsening of three dimensional anisotropic unstructured meshes for multigrid applications
citation.author: Mesri
citation.unstructured_citation: Mesri, Y., Guillard, H., & Coupez, T. (2012). Automatic coarsening of three dimensional anisotropic unstructured meshes for multigrid applications. Applied Mathematics and Computation, 218, 10500–10519. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.amc.2012.04.014
